chapter_title.ar: باب طَلَاقِ الَّتِي لَمْ تَبْلُغْ وَ الَّتِي قَدْ يَئِسَتْ مِنَ الْمَحِيضِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 24 – Divorce of the woman who has yet to mature, and one who has despaired from the menstruation (menopause)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/145)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/145)
text: الصَّبِيَّةِ الَّتِي لَا تَحِيضُ مِثْلُهَا وَ الَّتِي قَدْ يَئِسَتْ مِنَ الْمَحِيضِ قَالَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِمَا عِدَّةٌ وَ إِنْ دُخِلَ بِهِمَا .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Mahboub, from Hammad Bin Usman,

(It has been narrated) reporting from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding the young girl the likes of whom do not menstruate, and the woman who has despaired from the menstruation (in menopause). He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is no waiting period upon both of them, even if they had both been copulated with’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn Mahbub from Hammad ibn ‘Uthman from the one who narrated to him who has said the following: “About the case of a girl whose likes do not experience Hayd (menses) and a woman after menopause abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘They do not need to count waiting period even if he has gone to bed with them.”’
